mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance depresses equity valuations by raising the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year while simultaneously housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,200 thousand units annualized, indicating weakening consumer demand and housing investment. This joint deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings revisions turn negative.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth < 2% AND HOUST < 1200 thousand units
regime_hint: late-cycle demand weakness or early recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST conditions are met, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) expands at a rate exceeding 1.5% month-over-month, signaling robust manufacturing activity and supply-side capacity utilization. This sustained supply expansion supports corporate profit margins and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations upward.
regime_hint: any regime with positive supply momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of months with INDPRO MoM growth > 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized in the most recent quarter while inflation-adjusted retail sales remain flat or negative, indicating demand-side stagnation. This dual weakness predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as recession risk premia increase.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 1.5% AND RSAFS shows no growth or contraction YoY
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown or early recession regime
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 62% of instances when both GDPC1 and RSAFS conditions are met, or interaction term fails to improve model fit (R² increase < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 50,000 jobs per month while the unemployment rate remains stable or rises, signaling labor market slack and reduced wage pressure. This combination produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as inflation expectations decline and real discount rates fall.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth < 50,000 AND UNRATE stable or rising
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 158,543 thousand (vintage) / 157,608 thousand (revised) as of 2024-05-01
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle following labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month rolling window while the federal funds rate remains above 4.5%, signaling that monetary tightening is successfully reducing price pressures. This creates expectations for future rate cuts, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity risk premia compress.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when both conditions are met, or interaction term does not improve model fit (R² increase < 0.015) over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) expand above 1,500 thousand units annualized while real retail sales (RSAFS) grow at 3% or higher year-over-year, indicating robust consumer demand and residential investment. This dual strength produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as forward earnings expectations rise and cyclical equity valuations expand.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1500 thousand units AND RSAFS YoY growth >= 3%
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with strong demand momentum
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 52% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 2-year rolling window.
